commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `chat.send` dispatch-error cleanup to persist a failed session lifecycle event, emit `sessions.changed`, and add a WebChat Gateway regression test.

PR surface: Source +65, Tests +67. Total +132 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's `chat.send` dispatch rejection path broadcasts an error and removes the active run without persisting a failed lifecycle state. I did not run the failing scenario in this read-only review, but the current source path and added regression test make the stale `running` plus `hasActiveRun:false` shape clear.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is appropriate because the PR is maintainer-labeled and the remaining blocker is session-state merge sequencing plus final-head validation, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Fix WebChat dispatch failure session status
target.body: ## Summary - Persist a terminal failed session lifecycle event when WebChat dispatch rejects before an assistant reply. - Preserve terminal lifecycle errors from embedded-agent/provider failures when model fallback is exhausted, so fallback assistant text no longer clears the pending failure before the grace timer finalizes the session. - Broadcast the matching `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "failed"` and accurate `hasActiveRun` so Control UI can clear the active run state. - Add regression tests for pre-agent dispatch rejection, aborted dispatch rejection, embedded lifecycle-error finalization, exhausted fallback handling, and legacy session-key persistence. ## Root Cause The WebChat `chat.send` async dispatch error path cleaned up the active run and broadcast a chat error, but it did not update persisted session lifecycle state. That left `sessions.list` able to return `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: false`, which kept the Control UI in a permanent running state. For the real embedded-agent provider rejection path, the embedded run emitted `lifecycle:error`, but a later non-terminal fallback/assistant event cleared the pending terminal lifecycle error before the grace timer finalized it. The session therefore stayed persisted as `running` even though the active run had ended. ## Validation - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts` - `pnpm tsgo:test:src` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts` ## Real behavior proof - Behavior addressed: WebChat sessions no longer remain `status: "running"` with no active run after an embedded AIGW provider 400 before reply. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR worktree using `atlassian-aigw/openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352`. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: started `openclaw-local-test --repo /Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw-pr-84352-review --state-dir ~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final --model openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352 --no-channels --ttl 2h`, sent a `chat.send` WebSocket request to the local Gateway, then waited for the lifecycle grace window. - Evidence after fix: local logs showed `400 "Bad Request"`, `LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.`, and `Embedded agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.` - Observed result after fix: `~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final/agents/super/sessions/sessions.json` showed the affected session with `status: "failed"`, non-null `endedAt`, and non-null `runtimeMs`. - What was not tested: Slack/Discord channel delivery; local proof used WebChat/Gateway only with channels disabled. - Video evidence:captures the fixed WebChat run-status behavior locally. This is local evidence and is not committed. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c69aab1f-fb02-44fb-bcdf-c3355909004e
target.author: jesse-merhi
